published.title: Home
published.content: 

published.title: About
published.content: WHY? To remind people of who we are and who we can be collectively so that together we can give each other support, meaning, purpose and joy. HOW? By telling the story of "these days us" through music. WHAT? * Songs & Recordings * Concerts, Live performances * Other formats such as videos, drawings etc. BIO Erik Sjøholm believes in the vulnerable and fragile human being, in the encounters along our path and the impact we have on each other. Music has the ability to connect and evoke emotions, to revive memories and the stories that make us who we are. This music, carries the color and impact of all the people Erik has met along his own path. Every third Friday of the month he celebrates the release of a new song, a new insight and story from his own life. His sound is influenced by various

published.title: Erik Sjøholm - Discography listed
published.content: Sooner or later - single LINKS: SONG INFO BUY / STREAM Matsawana - single PRESS RELEASE Release date: 17.01.2010 Artist: Erik Sjøholm Composed by: Erik Sjøholm & Timothée Dorr Lyrics by: Erik Sjøholm ISRC: FIEDS2000001 The nomadic singer-songwriter Erik Sjøholm (FIN) believes in the vulnerable and fragile human being, in the encounters along our path and the impact we have on each other. With his new tropical/world single Matsawana he invites his audience to reflect on our relationship to death. "Matsawana represents the presence of death we learn to live with from the moment we're born until the day we pass away." — Erik Sjøholm Recorded at: Madrid/Vasa/Kvevlax Producer/label: Erik Sjøholm Mixed by: Miguel Díaz Mastered by: Miguel Díaz Cover art by: Erik Sjøholm Recording artists: Lead vo

published.title: Erik Sjøholm - Biography
published.content: Erik Sjøholm grew up in a small village on the west coast of Finland, northern Europe. Inspired by music from the 60’s and the 70’s with artists like Paul Simon, Bruce Springsteen, Joni Mitchell, and John Lennon, Erik started writing music at an early age and today he is based in Madrid, focused on a solo career as a singer-songwriter, writing uplifting and energetic songs with soul/roots sound, slightly touching the world music genre at times, transmitting a relaxed, feel good vibe. The next release by Erik to look forward to is a single release, the 20th of September, with the title "In the beginning", written by Erik Sjøholm & Sebastian Jacobson and recorded at Nordic Audio Labs in Finland. Erik Sjøholm (FIN) “A Nordic Singer-Songwriter with the soul of Leon Bridges, African vibe of Pau

published.title: Bonfire stories
published.content: “ Bonfire stories ” is a collaboration between Erik Sjøholm and Lina Teir, mixing acoustic, raw and intimate music performances with storytelling. The concert offers an opportunity to reflect on our relationship to ourselves and others, resolving old tensions and emotions along the passing of the concert, by burning them in the bonfire (only literally if the venue offers the possibility of a bonfire, otherwise metaphorically). Sjøholm and Teir are both born and raised in Ostrobothnia, Finland, a flatland nordic landscape where you’ll see northern lights during winter and a never setting sun during summer. Even though coming from the same area it wasn’t until last year (2018) Lina and Erik came together in various collaborations and started exploring their artistic expression through improv

published.title: Lost and found
published.content: We are perfect and complete when we are born. However, when walking through life, we tend to forget this and end up feeling lost. This acoustic concert offers the listener a possibility of looking into the “lost and found” basket and rediscover things we’ve left behind. Every song talks about a specific insight or topic and the music stretches from a folky sound to soul and pop. On the stage you'll find Erik Sjøholm, a man with an impressive voice and an acoustic guitar. This is a very raw and intimate show which could vary between 30min to 3 hours depending on the venue. Erik has a calm and relaxed stage presence and brings feelgood, chill vibes with his music, similar to James Bay, Jack Johnson. Find all related promo materials etc. to the "Lost and found" tour below. *** JTNDYSUyMGRhdGE

published.title: In the beginning
published.content: Reviews “As I'm a veteran Motown staffwriter myself, I know the "real deal" musically when I hear it. And this latest song from Erik "In The Beginning" has got "soul, soul, soul, for days !!!! I had a bounce in my step and a big old smile on my face from.....beginning...to end !!!” Alan Roy Scott, top Hollywood songwriter and music producer "An energetic, uplifting track calling to mind '80's pop sophisticates like Spandau Ballet" Brett Perkins, Founder of Copenhagen Songwriters Festival Info Song title: In the beginning Music & Lyrics by: Erik Sjøholm & Sebastian Jacobson ISRC code: GBMJG1916598 Recorded at: Nordic Audio Labs Produced by: Erik Sjøholm Mixed by: Markus Sjöberg Mastered by: Eric Broyhill (Monsterlab Audio) Recording artists: Drumset: Daniel Hjerppe Percussion: Oscar Karlsso

published.title: Sooner or later
published.content: Erik Sjøholm against stress The nomadic singer-songwriter Erik Sjøholm (FIN) looks at stress as one of the most common illnesses in our western society. He believes in the impact we have on each other and presents his new relaxing and uplifting single "sooner or later" to fight the negative and offer people a possibility to release tension. INFO Song title: Sooner or later Music composed by: Erik Sjøholm Lyrics written by: Erik Sjøholm ISRC code: FIEDS2000002 Recorded at: Madrid/Vasa/Kvevlax Producer/label: Erik Sjøholm Mixed by: Miguel Díaz Mastered by: Miguel Díaz Cover art by: Erik Sjøholm Recording artists: Lead vocals, Backing vocals, Guitars, Keyboards, Percussion: Erik Sjøholm Bass: Mats Ödahl Percussion: Tuukka Aitoaho Violin: Santiago Vokram Sooner or later by Erik Sjøholm was pub

published.title: #storytelling #singer/songwriter #generationaltrauma
published.content: GLENN "The Things We Don't Speak About" A Storytelling Concert by Erik Sjøholm https://youtu.be/wgqBaPS2KSk About "When I was born, the same day, almost at the same time, my uncle Glenn decided to take his own life. Why? Sometimes we are left with nothing but a blank page." In this storytelling concert, nordic singer/songwriter & storyteller Erik Sjøholm dives deep into his own mythology, exploring the stories that have shaped his life. Taking inspiration from ancient myths, critical twentieth century authors and current singer/songwriters like Regina Spector and Bob Dylan, Erik invites the audience to an intimate and raw live experience that flows seamlessly through songs and stories to a place where we can discover new things about ourselves and why we do the things that we do. Technical

published.title: Website updates! Getting back on track!
published.content: Hello Folks! Step by step I´m building up my website again after it broke down and everything was deleted some weeks ago! Hope you´re having a great day! Keep learning, keep smiling!

published.title: Greetings from Italy
published.content: Hello everyone! Greetings from Italy, Padova to be more precise. Here we are, me and Tami. Visiting our "La latina" family members Ali and Luka, that we met during our stay in Madrid, September-February last year. Here we are learning a lot about the Italian way of life. Expressions, ways of thinking, traditions, sharing life experiences etc. Central aspects of life here is certainly the food. Fresh food, following the fruit/vegetable of the season. Right now, the mellon is hot on the table and we enjoy it so much almost to every meal we eat. We´ve been learning a lot about family and what friendship really holds. Our friendship to Ali and Luka has grown even stronger and it´s a pleasure to meet their families and discover where they come from, where they´ve grown up. Parents, siblings, gr

published.title: Thankful
published.content: I'm so thankful for all My friends and family around. Thank you for being you <3

published.title: New friends from Belgium
published.content: 10.7.2017, in the Van, scorze-padova Hello dear friends! Sending a greeting from the road. Yesterday we've met and made new friends from Belgium. They are making a holiday trip with their renault van that they have turned into a caravan. I'm very impressed by how they have done it and get inspired by the thought of it. Let's see if this could be something for the near future to come for Tami and me!? They've also shared nice stories from travelling different parts of the world. They specifically highlight their experiences in south-america, going around in the same style, with a 4x4 truck, for one year. They share about tribes, nature, the salt lake, dangers, what to think about etc. Very interesting and inspiring! Today we'll go on a field trip to the hills of Padova and explore the histo

published.title: Inspiration for "Compromise"
published.content: Today I will share a big inspiration to the arrangement of My song "Compromise". D'angelo is a big and important artist for rhythm music and when hearing this track from his last album "Black Messiah" I completely fell in love with it and that love also gave it's influence on my on recordings later, especially the song "Compromise". Ladies and gentlemen, this is: Really Love Really love - D'angelo, Black Messiah #reallylove #Dangelo #music #blackmessiah #inspiration #recordings #records

published.title: To share, care and respect
published.content: Hello sunshine, Tonight I want to take a moment and think out loud about love. I´m sitting in a room, in a house located in "La Ferte, sous Jouarre", the outskirts of Paris. Why I´m here is a different story but right now I want to focus and explore the topic love. They say love is the greatest of all the powers and it strikes me in the most difficult moments, how true this is for me. When I try to define what love is for me I print: Love is shared. Love is to care. Love is to respect. Love is so many other values but these are the three first ones that come to me. I was sitting down taking time together with Tami, my partner, friend, and almost wife to clarify and update our current aims the other day and after a couple of hours of defining I reached this sentence: "To express love throug

published.title: Monday news, La Ferte sous-jouarre, France 30.10
Your message is here
published.content: Your message is here. I am 25 years old, feeling alive and well. They say I’m in the best of days so I figured as new years is approaching, why not write down a few notes each day about how the best of my days went. Which is why I present these words with the title “Your message is here”. This is my latest discovery and the best thing I know to share with you right now. Since I started songwriting and especially writing lyrics, I’ve been in search of my message. What do you want to share with the whole wide world if you’d get the chance? What will be worth saying? What will be important enough to bother people with? These question and this amount of pressure made me eventually hate writing lyrics but since being a singer-songwriter very much includes being clear on your own message and hav

published.title: An ocean of opportunities
published.content: An ocean of opportunities To be an artist these days is like swimming in an ocean of opportunities. There are so many ways to go about it and yet there’s only one way for you to go about it - your way. My current problem is that people don’t listen to my music. This may be for several reasons. However, the once that do listen to my music keep making remarks on how much they enjoy it so it doesn’t seem to be a problem of quality. The feedback that I receive is rather that it was hard to find or that it’s weird they haven’t heard about it before. Receiving this feedback I conclude my main problem to be that people aren’t discovering my music in the places where they discover music in their daily lives. They simply don’t find it. And I do admit that I don’t make much of an effort to share abo

published.title: Zodiac Signs
published.content: Zodiac signs Some days are worse, other better. I do admit having stress around productivity and measuring my life in how productive I was that day. If I did a lot and accomplished a lot, I tend to say “today was a good day”. Other days that doesn’t end up a measurable result, I tend to sigh about and conclude as “today wasn’t a day worth anything at all”. These conclusions do have a big impact on my life and especially on the tempo, in which I live my life in. The pressure of achieving and “earning” a good day tends to max the expectations and pace of the day and I end up in a constant loop of developing projects, creating and solving new problems and raising the expectations of what I should achieve in order to conclude my day as a “good day”. I see this pattern and behavior in fellow mu

published.title: I am nobody
published.content: I am nobody Can you relate if I say that at times I feel like I’m a drop in the ocean, dust in the wind? Our system is built like a pyramid, everyone fighting for a place in the top. But how many can fit there? A few sure can, and I’m gonna make sure to be one of them. Because if I’m not reaching the top. Who am I? Nobody? Am I gonna live my whole life to end up being nobody? My whole life just a waste? What should I accomplish to earn a place at the top? And what will I do when I finally reach the top? What changes and differences will I make for the world? How many people will end up where I am now.. being nobody. A drop in the ocean. Dust in the wind. I will turn back to what I wrote a few days ago. It’s all about perspective. How do you want your life to be? How can you make it real? W

published.title: Short clip from our performance in Festival "In Situ", 26.5 Paris, France
12.10.2018 | La Fortuna, Costa Rica
published.content: Right now it's 00.00 as I'm writing these lines. I'm at hostel "La Choza Inn" in La Fortuna in Costa Rica and about to close this day and go to sleep. It's been an amazing day, we've made a good hike and I've made many new friends today. In a group of five we took a taxi upp to "Cerro Chato" and climbed all the way to the green lagun that lies right in the crater of the now dead Volcano. It was a sight of a lifetime and for anyone visiting I strongly recommend you to go there to see it for yourself. It's a challenging track but if you take it Slow it will be allright. When coming back home we made dinner together and shared delicious glutenfree pasta with cheese, cheery tomatoes and mushrooms all mixed with green pesto! Yummy!! And the talks is something I really appreciate now Looking bac

published.title: San José, Costa Rica
published.content: Travel day today. We're heading southwest to of Costa Rica down to a place called "Drake Bay". We're gonna spend the next few weeks doing a workaway.info volunteering at a hotel called "Mohagine". It's right at the beach and we're hoping to be able to spot both whales, sea turtles and lots of Wildlife. Drake Bay is located right next to a big natural park called "Corcovado" that we've heard many good things about so we're very exited for it. During our stay there apart from spotting awesome animals and learning new things I will take time for songwriting and recording. I've had a lot of impressions the last week and it's time to reflect and express them now. That's all for now, talk soon!!

published.title: Drake Bay, Costa Rica
published.content: 27.10.2018 Hello, Well today has been a day of ups and downs for sure. I've been reflective today and climbed some mountains when thinking about how to make future projects and dreams come true. Sometimes when you think too big and too far in the future, everything becomes overwhelming and you find yourself feeling helpless and so small that you couldn't even take the first step of the hike to the top. I was here at that place today. I have certain goals with my music career and I'm kind of building my life around it so in a way, it has to work.. hehe.. do you have that feeling too? At least this is the kind of pressure I put on myself but once you take the first step and start walking it always clears up and the skies grow wider for every step. Voila, I'll drop it off here. Today has been

published.title: La Latina, Lyrics
published.content: Here's the lyrics for my most recent release "La Latina. A song that was born in Madrid written as a Christmas gift to my flat mates. When I sing this song these days it goes out to my "world family". To all of you guys. Listen to "La Latina" on my youtube channel. Cheers!

published.title: Arrival in Santa Marta, Colombia
published.content: 6.11.2018 Today we are in Santa Marta, Colombia. We arrived here after a night at the airport in Bogota because of flight troubles when coming from San José. The immigration center was simply crammed with people which caused us to miss our flight. No worries though, the flight company easily got us new tickets for the next flight that would leave early, the next morning. So here we are now, at ”La Bella Samaria” almost in the center of the oldest city in the whole south-america. Hopefully we can stay three weeks here (volunteering) and discover all the secrets and stories this city has to tell. As a summary of todays first impressions I have a good feeling, nice people and crazy vibe in town. My eyes are getting so tired and my body is screaming for sleep, so off I go. Take care buddy, E

published.title: First impressions: Colombia
published.content: 7.11.2018 Colombia. There’s music everywhere. People are singing when they speak. The sun beats hard at mid day. Our work is social and we already got the hang of it. Food is cheap, most prices we see are cheap. The currency is called pesos here, 1€ is around 3000 pesos, más o menos. We are making several new friends each day, creating arts and things and coming up with many new ideas. It’s close to midnight here, what about over there? Will go to bed saying that this was a good day 🌞 take care!

published.title: A night of street art
published.content: 9.11.2018 Hey, How was your day? Here it’s getting hotter and hotter as the summer is approaching here in Colombia. Santa Marta is said to be the hottest city of Colombia and I start to realize what’s behind those words.. tonight we took a walk around the city to find some live music. We mainly found street acts strumming away, banging on drums while singing their hearts out. There’s certainly a lot of rhythm in these folks, right at the start we saw a group of 4 kids performing in front of the restaurants. Very inspiring night. When we got back to ”La Bella Samaria” I changed the strings on my guitar, ready to rock myself to sleep now. Hasta manana!

published.title: 6.1.2019, Riohacha, Colombia
published.content: Hey, Gosh, right now I’m Living an experience that is too intense for me to have time with social media. I’m in Riohacha, working with a fair trade foundation and it’s requiring my 100% all the time. Learning a lot and growing as a person, there is even music and a beautiful song coming out of this experience, so you will hear about it in meantime. But here I am, I didn’t forget you if that’s what you think! I love you all and send you a big hug on this Sunday morning in Colombia!

published.title: 27.1 Colombia comes to an end
published.content: Hello world! Today the Colombian chapter will come to an end. We’re flying to Peru in an hour and we’re sooo exited!! Three months in Colombia has taught me a lot, made me grow as a person and given me many unique perspectives on life. Colombia has lived hard times for a long time which you can sense in the people, the streets, the art and overall atmosphere in the country. For anyone thinking of visiting Colombia I def. say; yes! Go ahead! And pay attention to your surroundings! Next time I write it will be from Peru! Yeiy!

published.title: 3.2.2018 in Cuzco, Peru
published.content: Massive attack Cage the elephant Marabou state Young fathers These are bands and artists that my new friend @Michaelfungphotography from India has photographed and recommended me to listen to. It’s been an eventful day. We got up at seven in the morning and went directly to a yoga festival, between 08.00-12.00 we did four classes of yoga in a row! A bit crazy but def. worth it. After a four plated lunch we had a coffe and a waffle with icecream, strawberries, blueberries and bananas. That’s how much you can eat after four hours of yoga! Later at our current home we watched the incredibly beautiful movie seven days in Tibet with Brad Pitt. What beautiful messages that movie shares. Impressive. I’m very happy and thankful for the people that show up in my life right now. They inspire me and

published.title: What an unexpected day
published.content: 21.2.2019 We’ve walked in the mountains, sung mantras, planted apricot seeds, developed the concept of Bonfire stories with Lina Teir & co. over whatsApp, eaten a wooden cooked pizza that was incredibly tasty, I also got bitten by a dog (didn’t go through my pants though so nothing dangerous), we helped a drunk man get back home, we’ve reflected on communication and on time, how to add ”nothing” to your schedule so that your heart have time to show you where to go next, these days feels like weeks, that’s how eventful my life is. What can I say, couldn’t be happier! Sending lots of love to you guys!

published.title: Stillness, observation and patience
published.content: Hey, how are you friend? I am well, on the move again. For the next 15 days we'll be changing location almost every day, so we have some quite intense traveling and exploring days ahead of us. Today we've visited the salt mine of Maras, the inka laboratories of Moray and finally Pisaq, which is an old inka city. When closing the day with Tami, reflecting about everything we've experienced today I sum up with an impression of stillness, observation and patience. Sometimes when traveling or simply living our day to day life we loose ourselves in the external world.. today I did loose myself a lot to the external world, however, when looking inside of me, I found these imprints from todays exposure; stillness, observation and patience. The process of creation, of learning and exploring someth

published.title: Easily we forget..
published.content: Here in Europe, we talk a lot about work. We identify a lot with our working title. Easily we look at ourselves as ”teacher”, ”doctor”, ”artist”, this and that.. labels. Easily we forget that there is so much more to life than work and that we are so much more than our working titles. Easily we forget many things about life.

published.title: Bonfire Stories, Day 1
published.content: 16.4.2019 Today ”Bonfire Stories” will host the first concert of the spring tour through Europe. It will take place nowhere else but in my hometown/village where I’ve grown up, here in Malax. I’m very exited for what awaits us and I hope to see many of you who live in this area tonight at Malakta. Let’s start this day and rock it!! 😍

published.title: Bonfire Stories, After the first show...
published.content: 17.4.2019 All went well! Our first concert is checked and finnished, tomorrow head on to no. 2 in Henriksdal where Lina has grown up. Feeling very proud and satisfied with todays gig and all the beautiful people that came out to listen. Also very happy that @ kksel and Ia Udd joined our show today. Thank you! Now let’s have a few hours of sleep! More updates tomorrow, nati nati!

ambiguous.title: Sooner or later
ambiguous.content: Erik Sjøholm against stress The nomadic singer-songwriter Erik Sjøholm (FIN) looks at stress as one of the most common illnesses in our western society. He believes in the impact we have on each other and presents his new relaxing and uplifting single "sooner or later" to fight the negative and offer people a possibility to release tension.
